children.label: A
children.text: stating that the order, a provision of the order, or an obligation imposed in connection with the order is not established in accordance with law; and

children.label: B
children.text: requesting a modification of the order or obligation or an exemption from the order or obligation.

children.heading: Statute of limitations
children.label: 2 Statute of limitations
children.text: A petition under paragraph (1) concerning an obligation may be filed not later than 2 years after the date of imposition of the obligation.

children.heading: Hearings
children.label: 3 Hearings
children.text: The petitioner shall be given the opportunity for a hearing on a petition filed under paragraph (1), in accordance with regulations issued by the Secretary.

children.heading: Ruling
children.label: 4 Ruling
children.text: After a hearing under paragraph (3), the Secretary shall issue a ruling on the petition that is the subject of the hearing, which shall be final if the ruling is in accordance with applicable law.

children.heading: Commencement of action
children.label: 1 Commencement of action
children.text: The district court of the United States for any district in which a person who is a petitioner under subsection (a) resides or carries on business shall have jurisdiction to review a ruling on the petition, if the person files a complaint not later than 20 days after the date of issuance of the ruling under subsection (a)(4).

children.heading: Process
children.label: 2 Process
children.text: Service of process in a proceeding under paragraph (1) may be made on the Secretary by delivering a copy of the complaint to the Secretary.

children.label: A
children.text: to make such ruling as the court shall determine to be in accordance with law; or

children.label: B
children.text: to take such further proceedings as, in the opinion of the court, the law requires.

children.heading: Enforcement
children.label: c Enforcement
children.text: The pendency of proceedings instituted under subsection (a) may not impede, hinder, or delay the Secretary or the Attorney General from taking action under .section 7487 of this title
